metadata.title:
SOCIALISTS ASK


FOR RELIEF

WANT CITY TO PROVIDE AS
MUCH PUBLIC WORK AS
POSSIBLE FOR IDLE MEN


AND BOOST PUBLIC MARKET

pages.raw_text:
In petition presented the mayor
today Spokane socialists ask that
as much public work az possible be
sstarted, so as to give employment
!, ". the hundreds of idle men with
= Nch the city is flooded. The pc.
y ION says that the men who make
little wages at the best are sorely
In need of work, are willing to
work and are out Of employment
through no fault of their own, but
by reason of the bank panic.


In reference to the public work
plan the mayor said that this is
the wrong time of year for city
work, and the city treasury has
no more money for public improve
ments.


In another communication which
leading socialists signed. Mayor
Moore was asked to lend his aid to-
wards the immediate construction
of public market, to be located
at the west of the south end of the
Monroe st. bridge on the north side
of Riverside av., extending north
upon concrete piers. A market
large enough for farmers to drive
their loads into is advocated. Ow-
ing to the lack of space enough at
the suggested location on Front av.
